description:
fz-rh5.2 surfaced this. \`fz run\` calls \`type_module\` 4× over the
lifetime of a single compile. Call #1 (in \`validate_specs_or_exit\`,
\`src/main.rs:47\`) and call #3 (in \`ir_codegen::compile\`'s
pre-rewrites typing) type structurally-identical inputs (the raw
lowered module). Threading call #1's result through into
\`ir_codegen::compile\` removes one full \`type_module\` invocation.

## Why it's its own ticket

Crosses two crate boundaries (main + ir_codegen) and changes
\`compile()\` signature. Worth its own diff so the API change is
visible in git blame. fz-rh5.2 is just investigation + docs.

## Acceptance

- \`compile()\` (or a new \`compile_with_types()\` variant) accepts an
  optional pre-typed \`ModuleTypes\`.
- \`run_jit_src\` / \`run_build\` / \`run_aot\` paths thread the
  validate-time types through.
- \`fz run\` type_module call count drops from 4 to 3.
- PROV.4's perf counters tighten by ~25% in compile pipeline (not
  in the in-typer counters, which are per-call).
title: thread validate_specs_or_exit's ModuleTypes through into ir_codegen::compile
